description:
You are an on-call operations engineer who has to triage an incident that
    requires running a diagnostic script inside its own, clean Python
    environment.  
    Your goal is to:

    1. Create a brand-new Python virtual environment, using the standard
       Python “venv” module.
    2. Place the virtual environment at the exact path  
          /home/user/.ops/venvs/incident2024
       (create any missing parent directories if required).
    3. After the environment is successfully created, generate a one-line
       log file named  
          /home/user/incident2024_setup.log
       that contains **exactly one line**, ending with a single newline
       character “\n”, whose text is the absolute path to the Python
       interpreter inside the virtual environment.

       Concretely, the file content must be:

          /home/user/.ops/venvs/incident2024/bin/python\n

       (There must be no additional spaces, no surrounding quotes, and no
       extra lines before or after that line.)

    4. Do not activate the environment permanently; just ensure it is
       created and that the log file is written in the required format.

    The automated grader will verify all of the following:

    • Directory /home/user/.ops/venvs/incident2024 exists.  
    • File /home/user/.ops/venvs/incident2024/bin/python exists and is
      executable.  
    • File /home/user/incident2024_setup.log exists and its **entire**
      content is the single line shown above (followed by a newline).  

    REMEMBER:  
    • Do not write anything else to the log file.  
    • Do not include comments or additional output in the file.  
    • Use only regular user privileges.  
    • You may display whatever console output you like, but the filesystem
      state must match the specification exactly.
truth:
<!-- The grader will look for the following final state -->

    <!-- 1. Virtual-environment directory -->
    The path /home/user/.ops/venvs/incident2024 must exist and be a
    directory.  Within it, the sub-directory bin/ must exist and contain the
    Python interpreter named “python”.

    <!-- 2. Interpreter path -->
    The interpreter must be located at  
        /home/user/.ops/venvs/incident2024/bin/python  
    and must have the executable bit set.

    <!-- 3. Verification log -->
    File /home/user/incident2024_setup.log must exist with mode 0644 (or
    more restrictive) and contain exactly the single line

        /home/user/.ops/venvs/incident2024/bin/python\n

    No extra whitespace or extra newlines are permitted.

    The grader will fail the task if:
    • The virtual-environment directory or its Python interpreter is missing.
    • The log file is missing or its contents deviate in any character,
      including missing or extra newline characters.
name: task_000000_76890809
